title: Quick-Fix request
description: A per-session, structured Quick-Fix authorization. NOTE: this file is an ATTESTATION + a machine-verifiable scope declaration; it is NOT a signed, tamper-proof credential and `human_activation:true` does not prove activation. The REAL activation action is a human explicitly running the Quick-Fix launcher (or explicitly instructing the current session to run it) — see process/quickfix-lane.md. A malformed/missing request fails closed: the lane is NOT entered. Normative source: process/quickfix-lane.md; if this schema and that spec disagree, the spec wins.

request_id.description: Git-ref-/filesystem-safe slug. Names the quickfix/<request_id> branch, the ephemeral worktree, and the bundle. No slashes.

created_by.description: Human who authored the request (attestation; not an identity proof).

human_activation.description: ATTESTATION ONLY — the human asserts they are authorizing this Quick Fix. It is not unforgeable and is NOT the activation mechanism (running the launcher is). Required `true` so a missing/false value fails closed.

harness.description: Target coding-agent harness id (e.g. claude_code). The launcher fails closed unless this harness is marked `supported` in the harness-support registry (adapter delivered + cold-start evidence recorded).

task_summary.description: What the fix is, in one or two sentences. Ambiguous/empty summaries fail the minLength gate; vague natural language ('just tidy up') is not a Quick-Fix authorization.

allowed_globs.description: Human-approved scope. The guard enforces that EVERY touched path (rename source AND destination) matches at least one of these. Repo-relative POSIX globs only.

items.description: Repo-relative POSIX glob, SAFE SUBSET only: letters, digits, '.', '_', '-', '/', and the wildcards '*'/'**'/'?'. Character classes ('[...]'), brace expansion ('{...}'), commas, and negation ('!') are DISALLOWED because they can smuggle traversal/absolute semantics (e.g. '[.][.]', '{../x}', '[/]etc'). Also forbidden: absolute paths (leading '/'), '~', and '..' path segments. Adopter overlays may only ADD globs, never remove/exclude/override.

eligibility_attestation.description: Human attestations. These are NECESSARY preconditions the human asserts; they are NOT the enforcement gate (the deterministic guard + allowed_globs subset are). All must be true to launch.

targeted_verification.description: Structured verification command. Executed with shell=False and a bounded cwd (verify.py). argv[0] is checked against an executable allowlist; a non-allowlisted executable requires explicit extra human confirmation. Runs AFTER a preliminary guard and is followed by a FINAL guard.

argv.description: Argument vector. No shell string; no shell metacharacter interpretation.

cwd.description: Worktree-relative working dir for the verification. Must resolve inside the ephemeral worktree (enforced by verify.py); absolute/'..'/'~' rejected.

base_ref.description: Optional ref to branch the ephemeral worktree from. Default: the repo HEAD captured at launch (the baseline the guard binds to).

notes.description: Optional free-text context for the record/audit. Non-authoritative.
